topic.prompt_en: Describe your current daily life situation.
topic.learning_goal: Learn to summarize current daily life with routine, pressure points, balance, and future uncertainty.

angles.title: Give the overall picture
content: Describe your life in one balanced sentence first.
Words like busy, steady, transitional, or more balanced are useful.

angles.title: Describe the routine
content: Mention the main parts of your weekdays.
Do not try to narrate every hour.

angles.title: Mention one challenge
content: A current pressure point makes the answer realistic.
Keep it specific but not overly personal.

angles.title: End with direction
content: Say whether life feels stable, improving, or still uncertain.
A balanced ending sounds more mature than forced optimism.

sequence.target: Summarize your current stage of life in one sentence.
Explain what most days look like and where the pressure comes from.
End with how you see your life right now.

high_score_tips: Daily life answers sound stronger when you mix routine with interpretation.
Use moderate language such as fairly busy, a bit uncertain, or gradually more balanced.
Include one challenge, but keep the tone composed.
A realistic closing is often more impressive than a perfect-sounding life summary.

content.why_it_hurts: The answer sounds like a schedule instead of a life description.
content.fix: Summarize the routine, then explain how it feels or what phase you are in.

content.why_it_hurts: That often sounds artificial and less believable.
content.fix: Mention one challenge and one area that is getting better.

content.problem: No sense of change or direction
content.why_it_hurts: Current daily life sounds flat if there is no view on where things are heading.
content.fix: End with whether life feels stable, transitional, or still evolving.

language.wrong_example: My life is too busyly these days.
language.better_example: My life is quite busy these days.
language.reason: Busy is the correct adjective. Busyly is not a word.

language.wrong_example: I am adapting with this new schedule.
language.better_example: I am adapting to this new schedule.

core.example: My current schedule is fairly packed during the week.
I am trying to find a better balance between work and rest.
Most weekdays feel quite packed at the moment.
Even though things are busy, I still have a basic routine.
I am still adjusting to a slightly different rhythm.
Some parts of my life feel stable now, which I appreciate.

extended.note: Useful when life is not chaotic but not fully settled.

extended.item: carry a fair amount of responsibility
extended.note: Good for adult daily life answers.

extended.meaning_zh: 找到一个可持续的节奏
extended.note: Very practical phrase for current-life topics.
Good for honest but calm endings.

core.example: These days, my life feels quite structured but busy.
Most of my weekdays follow a similar pattern.

core.phrase: try to stay on top of things
core.example: I spend a lot of time trying to stay on top of things.
I am still trying to find a better balance between work and rest.
I would say I am in a transitional phase right now.
It is not perfect, but it is moving in the right direction.

extended.phrase: my days have more structure than before
extended.meaning_zh: 我的日子比以前更有结构感
extended.note: Useful when life is getting more organized.
extended.example: My days have more structure than before, which has helped a lot.

extended.phrase: carry a fair amount of responsibility
extended.note: Good for work, study, or family commitments.
extended.example: At the moment, I carry a fair amount of responsibility.
Things are better now, but I still have room to improve.

extended.meaning_zh: 找到一个可持续的节奏
extended.note: Strong phrase for the current-life topic.
extended.example: I am gradually finding a workable rhythm.

framework.goal: Describe your current daily life with structure, challenge, and a balanced sense of direction.

moves: Summarize your current life in one balanced sentence.
Avoid sounding either overly negative or unrealistically perfect.
Add one challenge or pressure point.
Explain whether life feels more stable, transitional, or still uncertain.

delivery_markers: If I had to sum it up, ...

samples.answer:
If I had to describe my current daily life, I would say it is quite busy but more organized than before. Most of my weekdays follow a similar routine, and that structure has been helpful for me.

I usually spend the first part of the day working, and later I deal with smaller tasks, exercise, or things at home. Sometimes it feels like there is always something waiting to be done, so I do feel under pressure from time to time. However, I have become better at planning my day and keeping my routine fairly stable.

So, even though life is not especially relaxed at the moment, it feels manageable. I still need to find a better balance between work and rest, but overall I think I am moving in the right direction.
highlights: Balanced opening with routine plus challenge

opening: If I had to describe my current daily life ...
body: I usually spend the first part of the day ...
closing: Overall, I think I am moving in the right direction.

upgrades.improved: My life is quite busy but more organized than before.
upgrades.why: This sounds more balanced and informative.

upgrades.original: I have a lot to do.
upgrades.improved: There is always something waiting to be done.
upgrades.why: This sounds more spoken and natural.

samples.answer:
At the moment, I would describe my daily life as fairly structured, but still a bit demanding. My days have more routine than they used to, which I appreciate, although the schedule can feel packed at times.

Most weekdays are divided between work, personal admin, and a few habits I try to protect, such as exercise or an evening walk. The main challenge is not chaos exactly, but the feeling that I am carrying a fair amount of responsibility and constantly trying to stay on top of things. That means I have to be more deliberate about rest than I used to be.

Overall, I think I am in a transitional phase. Life is gradually becoming more balanced, but it is not fully settled yet. Even so, I can feel that I am finding a more workable rhythm than before.
highlights: Good use of structured, demanding, and transitional phase
Stronger balance between routine and challenge

band_guide.focus: More nuanced summary of current life
opening: At the moment, I would describe my daily life as ...
My days have more routine than they used to ...
body: The main challenge is not chaos exactly, but ...
closing: Overall, I think I am in a transitional phase.

upgrades.improved: My schedule can feel packed, and I carry a fair amount of responsibility.
upgrades.why: This is more precise and more adult-sounding.

upgrades.improved: I am gradually finding a more workable rhythm.
upgrades.why: The idea becomes more specific and less vague.

samples.answer:
If I were to sum up my current daily life in one sentence, I would say it is stable enough to feel grounding, but still demanding enough that I have to manage it quite consciously. In other words, things are better organized now, though not effortless.

Most weekdays follow a fairly predictable pattern, which I actually value. I work through the main part of the day, deal with practical responsibilities, and then try to protect a small amount of time for movement, reading, or simply resetting. The challenge is that the days can still feel compressed, and if I am not careful, everything starts to blur into one long stream of obligations.

That said, I do not see this period negatively. I would describe it as a phase of adjustment rather than instability. I still have room to improve, especially in terms of rest and boundaries, but overall I feel that my life is gradually becoming more intentional and better balanced.
highlights: Band 8 answer balances stability, pressure, and direction
Intentional and better balanced are strong closing ideas

opening: If I were to sum up my current daily life ...
Things are better organized now, though not effortless.
body: Most weekdays follow a fairly predictable pattern ...
closing: I would describe it as a phase of adjustment ...
My life is gradually becoming more intentional ...

upgrades.improved: My life is stable enough to feel grounding, but still demanding enough that I have to manage it consciously.
upgrades.why: This captures complexity in a natural way.
upgrades.note: Useful for present-life topics with mixed feelings.

upgrades.improved: My life is gradually becoming more intentional and better balanced.
upgrades.why: The ending sounds more thoughtful and directional.
upgrades.note: A strong but realistic final sentence.

practice.task: Describe your current daily life situation and explain how balanced or demanding it feels right now.
checklist: Summarize your current life in one sentence.
Add one challenge and one direction or improvement.
self_prompts: How would you describe your life overall these days?
What do most weekdays look like?
Do things feel stable, transitional, or uncertain?
